dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.pauseAllConfirm: Are you sure you want to pause ALL active agents? This will immediately stop all agent operations.
dashboard.helpQuickControls: Agents using over 90% of their budget or in error state appear here. Use Pause All to immediately stop all agent operations in an emergency.

agents.title: Agents
agents.subtitle: Manage and monitor your AI agents
agents.noAgentsFiltered: Try adjusting your search or filters
agents.noAgentsYet: Create your first agent to get started
agents.killConfirmDesc: This will immediately stop the agent and revoke its API access. This action cannot be undone.
agents.killConfirmType: Type the agent name to confirm:
agents.deleteConfirmDesc: This will permanently delete the agent and all its data. This action cannot be undone.
agents.helpRateLimiting: Limits how many API requests this agent can process per minute. Leave empty to use the plan default.
agents.guardrailAutoPauseDesc: Automatically pause the agent when its budget limit is reached
agents.guardrailAutoDowngradeDesc: Switch to fallback model when primary model fails or budget is low
agents.guardrailSpikeDetectDesc: Auto-pause when cost exceeds 3x the rolling average
agents.noAuditLogsDesc: Changes to this agent will appear here
agents.addAgentDesc: Configure a new AI agent for your workforce
agents.presetDesc_conservative: Low limits, all safety features enabled. Best for experimental agents.
agents.presetDesc_standard: Balanced limits with auto-pause enabled. Good for most use cases.
agents.presetDesc_aggressive: High limits, minimal restrictions. For trusted, high-volume agents.
agents.presetDesc_custom: No preset limits applied. Configure guardrails manually after creation.
agents.helpBudgetLimits: Set maximum spend limits per day and month. When reached, the agent can be auto-paused or downgraded to a cheaper model.
agents.helpExecLimits: Control how long each task can run and how many tokens it can use per request. Prevents runaway costs from unexpectedly large prompts.
agents.helpAutomation: Automated safety features that trigger when limits are hit. These run on every API request through the proxy.

teams.title: Teams
teams.subtitle: Manage teams, members, and budgets
teams.noTeamsYet: Create your first team to get started
teams.deleteConfirmDesc: This will permanently delete the team. Agents will be unassigned. This action cannot be undone.
teams.deleteConfirmType: Type the team name to confirm:
teams.noAgentsDesc: Add agents to this team to see them here
teams.noMembersDesc: Add members to this team to manage access
teams.noAuditLogsDesc: Changes to this team will appear here

budget.title: Budget
budget.subtitle: Track and allocate budgets across teams and agents
budget.allocateSubtitle: Redistribute budgets across teams and agents
budget.helpTeamBudgets: Each team has a monthly budget that's split across its agents. The progress bar shows how much has been spent. Use the Allocate Budget page to redistribute.
budget.helpTimeline: Shows cumulative spending over the last 30 days. The dashed red line is your total budget limit. If the curve approaches the line, you may need to reduce spending or increase budget.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Cost analytics, agent performance, and ROI tracking
analytics.noROIDataDesc: Configure output values in agent settings to see ROI calculations. Set the value per output unit (e.g. $50 per lead) in agent metadata.
analytics.noRecommendationsDesc: No optimization recommendations at this time. Your agents are performing well.
analytics.recDowngradeDesc: This agent uses {from} but may perform well with {to} at lower cost.
analytics.recCostIncreaseDesc: Costs increased by {pct}% compared to the previous period.
analytics.recErrorDesc: {pct}% of tasks are failing. Check configuration or model choice.
analytics.recIdleDesc: No tasks in the last {days} days. Consider pausing to free budget.
analytics.recIdleNoTasks: This agent has no recorded tasks. Consider pausing to free budget.
analytics.recTeamBudgetDesc: {pct}% of budget used with {days} days remaining.

alerts.title: Alerts
alerts.subtitle: View and manage alerts for your AI workforce
alerts.noAlertsDesc: Alerts will appear here when agents trigger budget warnings, errors, or guardrails

profile.title: Profile
profile.subtitle: Manage your personal account settings
profile.emailReadonly: Email is managed through authentication settings
profile.twoFactorDesc: Add an extra layer of security to your account
profile.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
profile.notifCriticalDesc: Immediate email for budget exceeded, kill switch, and spike detection
profile.notifWarningDesc: Email when agents approach budget limits or failover occurs
profile.notifInfoDesc: Email for informational events like task completions
profile.notifDigestDesc: Summary of yesterday's activity, spending, and alerts at 9am
profile.exportDataDesc: Download a copy of all your personal data (GDPR compliant)
profile.exportRequested: Export requested. You will receive an email with the download link.
profile.deleteAccountDesc: Permanently delete your account and all personal data. This action cannot be undone.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage your organization, members, and preferences
settings.planDesc_pro: Up to 20 agents, unlimited teams, 50,000 requests/month
settings.planDesc_enterprise: Unlimited agents, unlimited requests, SSO, SLA
settings.deleteOrgDesc: Permanently delete this organization and all its data. This action cannot be undone.
settings.deleteOrgDisabled: Contact support to delete your organization
settings.roleOwnerDesc: Full access. Can manage billing, members, and delete the organization.
settings.roleAdminDesc: Manage agents, budgets, and settings. Cannot delete the organization.
settings.roleManagerDesc: View and pause/resume agents. Cannot change budgets or settings.
settings.roleViewerDesc: Read-only access to dashboards and analytics.
settings.removeMemberConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this member?
settings.notifCriticalDesc: Immediate email for budget exceeded, kill switch, and spike detection
settings.notifWarningDesc: Email when agents approach budget limits or model failover occurs
settings.notifInfoDesc: Email for informational events like task completions
settings.notifDigestDesc: Summary of yesterday's activity, spending, and alerts at 9am
settings.providersDesc: Manage your OpenAI, Anthropic, and other LLM provider connections
settings.apiKeysDesc: Manage API keys for the proxy endpoint
settings.integrationDesc: Connect your AI agents through the OpenAI-compatible proxy
settings.createKeyDesc: Generate a new API key for proxy access
settings.keyRevealWarning: Copy this key now. It won't be shown again.
settings.deleteKeyDesc: This will permanently revoke this API key. Any services using it will stop working.
settings.noKeysDesc: Create an API key to start using the proxy
settings.intQuickStartDesc: Complete these steps to start tracking your AI agents
settings.intAllSet: You're all set! Your agents are being tracked.
settings.intIntegrationCodeDesc: Change 2 lines of code to route through AI Workforce Manager
settings.intCodeNote: Everything else in your code stays exactly the same.
settings.intN8nStep6: Set Body (JSON) with your model and messages
settings.intMakeStep1: Add an HTTP module (Make a request)
settings.intMakeStep5: Set Body type to Raw → JSON
settings.intMakeStep6: Add your model and messages to the JSON body
settings.intApiResponseDesc: OpenAI Chat Completions format (even for Anthropic models)
settings.intRateLimitsDesc: Current limits based on your plan
settings.intHeaderLimit: Maximum number of requests allowed per window
settings.intHeaderRetry: Seconds until the rate limit resets
settings.intError401: Invalid or expired API key. Check that you're using your AWM key, not your OpenAI key.
settings.intError404: No active agent found. Ensure the API key is scoped to an active agent.
settings.intError429: Budget exceeded, rate limited, or plan limit reached. Check agent guardrails and plan limits.
settings.intError502: LLM provider unreachable. Check your provider connection and status.

providers.title: LLM Providers
providers.subtitle: Connect and manage your LLM provider API keys
providers.addProviderDesc: Connect a new LLM provider to use with your agents
providers.noProvidersDesc: Add an LLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) to start routing requests through the proxy
providers.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this provider? Agents using it may stop working.
providers.helpDisplayName: A friendly name to identify this provider connection. You can have multiple connections per provider (e.g. 'OpenAI Production' and 'OpenAI Backup').
providers.helpBaseUrl: Custom endpoint URL. Required for Azure OpenAI and custom providers. Leave empty for standard OpenAI/Anthropic/Google.
providers.helpRateLimit: Maximum requests per minute for this provider. Helps prevent rate limiting from the upstream API.

onboarding.welcomeSubtitle: Let's set up your workspace in 5 minutes.
onboarding.welcomeSkip: Skip setup, I'll explore on my own
onboarding.orgDesc: This is your workspace where you'll manage all your AI agents.
onboarding.orgSlugHint: Used in URLs and API references
onboarding.teamDesc: Teams help you organize agents and track budgets by department.
onboarding.agentDesc: An agent represents an AI workflow you want to track and control.
onboarding.providerDesc: We need your provider API key to proxy requests.
onboarding.providerWhyTitle: Why do we need your API key?
onboarding.providerWhyDesc: We use it to forward your agents' requests. Think of us as a smart proxy — your key stays encrypted, and you can revoke it anytime.
onboarding.providerKeyHint: Your key is encrypted and only used to proxy requests.
onboarding.integrateNote: Create an API key in Settings → API Keys after setup. Full integration guide available in Settings → Integration.
onboarding.doneDesc: Your AI workforce manager is ready. Here's what was set up:

docs.title: Help Center
docs.metaDesc: Complete guide to AI Workforce Manager — setup, integration, use cases, and troubleshooting
docs.heroSubtitle: Everything you need to connect your AI agents and start tracking costs, performance, and budgets.
docs.step1Desc: Sign up for free in 30 seconds. No credit card required. Your workspace is ready instantly.
docs.step2Desc: Add your OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google API key. We store it encrypted and use it to forward requests.
docs.step3Desc: Create a proxy API key. Your agents will use this instead of your provider key.
docs.step4Desc: Replace your api_key and base_url. Everything else stays the same. Really.
docs.step5Desc: Every request is now tracked with cost, tokens, and duration. Set budgets and guardrails from the dashboard.
docs.howSubtitle: Same response, now with full visibility
docs.howVisibilityDesc: Every API call tracked with cost, tokens, and duration. Know exactly what your agents are spending.
docs.howBudgetDesc: Set limits per agent, team, or organization. Auto-pause agents when they exceed their budget.
docs.howKillSwitchDesc: Stop runaway agents instantly, before they burn your budget. Spike detection catches loops automatically.
docs.setupStep1Desc: Sign up at the registration page. The free plan includes 3 agents and 1,000 requests per month.
docs.setupStep2Desc: Give us your OpenAI/Anthropic API key. We store it encrypted and use it to forward your agents' requests.
docs.setupStep2Providers: Supported providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google AI, Azure OpenAI, Custom endpoints
docs.setupStep2Test: Use the "Test Connection" button to verify your key works before saving.
docs.setupStep3Desc: This is NOT your OpenAI key. This is your AI Workforce Manager key. Your agents will use this instead.
docs.setupStep3Warning: Copy your key immediately — it's shown only once!
docs.setupStep4Highlight: You change exactly 2 things: api_key and base_url. Everything else stays the same.
docs.setupStep5Check3: Token count and cost are non-zero
docs.setupStep5Trouble: Something not right? Check the troubleshooting section below.
docs.codeThatsIt: That's it. Your agent now runs through AI Workforce Manager.
docs.useCase1Setup: 3 agents: Lead Finder, Lead Qualifier, Email Writer
docs.useCase3Roi: Spike detection caught a loop at ticket #47 and auto-paused the agent. Saved $200.
docs.faq1Q: Does the proxy slow down my agents?
docs.faq1A: Negligible overhead — typically <50ms added latency. The proxy forwards your request directly to the provider and streams the response back in real-time.
docs.faq2Q: What happens if the proxy is down?
docs.faq2A: Your agents will receive a 502 error. We recommend implementing a fallback to direct API calls in critical production systems. Our uptime target is 99.9%.
docs.faq3Q: Can I use multiple API keys per provider?
docs.faq3A: Yes! You can add multiple connections per provider (e.g., 'OpenAI Production' and 'OpenAI Backup'). The proxy uses the default provider first.
docs.faq4A: Yes. Streaming is fully supported. Set stream: true as usual. Token usage is tracked even for streamed responses.
docs.faq5Q: Do I need to rewrite my code?
docs.faq5A: No. You change exactly 2 lines: api_key and base_url. The proxy is fully OpenAI Chat Completions API compatible, even for Anthropic and Google models.
docs.faq6A: The free plan includes 3 agents and 1,000 requests/month. Pro ($49/mo) supports 20 agents and 50,000 requests. Enterprise ($199/mo) is unlimited.
docs.faq7Q: How secure is my API key?
docs.faq7A: Provider API keys are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before storage. Your AWM proxy keys use SHA-256 hashing. All connections use HTTPS.
docs.faq8Q: Can I use Azure OpenAI or custom endpoints?
docs.faq8A: Yes. When adding a provider, select 'Azure' or 'Custom' and enter your base URL. The proxy will forward requests to your custom endpoint.
docs.faq9Q: Do I need one proxy key per agent?
docs.faq9A: No. You can scope a key to a specific agent, or use one key for all agents. Scoped keys are recommended for better tracking.
docs.faq10A: GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, o3-mini, Claude Opus/Sonnet/Haiku, Gemini Pro/Flash, and any model your custom provider supports.
docs.faq11A: Yes. The GDPR data export feature is available in your profile settings. Task history and usage data can also be accessed via the dashboard.
docs.faq12A: Before each request, the proxy checks the agent's daily and monthly budgets. If exceeded, the request is rejected with a 429 error. You can enable auto-pause to stop the agent entirely.
docs.troubleDesc401: You're using the wrong API key. Use your AWM proxy key (awm_sk_...), NOT your OpenAI or Anthropic key.
docs.troubleDesc429Budget: Your agent has hit its budget limit. Increase the budget in the agent's Guardrails tab, or wait for the next period.
docs.troubleDesc429Rate: Too many requests per minute. Check your plan limits and agent rate limit guardrails. Upgrade your plan for higher limits.
docs.troubleDesc502: The LLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) is unreachable. Check the provider's status page and your provider connection in Settings.
docs.troubleDescNoTasks: Verify your base_url is correct and includes /api/v1 (not /api/v1/chat/completions). The SDK appends the endpoint path automatically.
docs.troubleDescZeroCost: This can happen if the model isn't in the pricing table. Check that you're using a supported model name exactly as listed.
